core_mechanism: spectral analysis of a random-matrix ensemble whose entries are a pairwise function of randomly placed points, showing a control-parameter-driven transition in the eigenvalue distribution from an all-positive (stable/vibrational) spectrum to one containing negative eigenvalues (unstable/saddle), signalled by spectral weight crossing zero
brief_reason: The abstract defines matrices as functions of random points in Euclidean space and reports a generic transition between a 'phonon' phase (positive, stable spectrum) and a 'saddle' phase (negative eigenvalues), which is precisely a control-driven change in the eigenvalue spectrum of a point-defined random matrix.
